t_ch: 点击任务栏图标
t_en: Click the icon on the taskbar

t_ch: 一个巨大的游戏窗口跳了出来，彻底覆盖了监控画面。
t_en: A huge game screen pops up, completely obscuring the surveillance images.

t_ch: 硕大的「失败」出现在屏幕正中央，血红色的字体诉说着刚才战斗的惨烈。
t_en: A large blood-red font appears in the center of the screen, announcing the tragic results of the battle: "Game Over."

t_ch: 居然输了，好可惜。
t_en: That's a shame.

t_ch: 「欢迎来到『黑塔』。」广告机的女声慵懒地说道，你感觉不到欢迎的意思。
t_en: "Welcome to Herta Space Station." You don't pick up any sense of welcome from the lazy and careless female voice coming out of the advertising bot.

t_ch: 「家用寻物仪，火热发售中！」广告机的女声慵懒地说道，你没有购买的欲望。
t_en: "Home-Use Object Finders, on sale now!" The advertising bot lazily declares, giving you no desire to purchase this.

t_ch: 「卡利白正统，就在卡利百！」广告机的女声慵懒地说道，你不懂两者有什么区别。
t_en: "The orthodox Kapo-Kali IS Kali-Kapo!" The advertising bot lazily declares, and you are confused about the differences between the two.

t_ch: 太空植物，你没见过，但很熟悉。
t_en: Some space plants. You've never seen them before, but they feel familiar.

t_ch: 这是一种原始色素体生物。
t_en: This is a primitive chromosomal-based organism.

t_ch: 这植物旁边的气味不太好闻，它制造的可能不是氧气。
t_en: This plant doesn't smell very nice. It may not be an oxygen producer.

t_ch: 一对垃圾桶，分类方式很怪异：诺布莱斯虫能吃的/诺布莱斯虫不能吃的。
t_en: A pair of trashcans with an unusual sorting method — whether or not the trash is digestible by Noblesse worms.

t_ch: 人体工学桌椅，可自适配对象包括人类、机械人偶、诺布莱斯虫、狐人、智械、呜呜伯、富翁、海因斯人、心灵感应蜘蛛、巴维鲁人、石头、狗、猫（普通）、猫（布偶）……
t_en: Ergonomic desks and benches that can be self-adjusted to cater to humans, robots, Noblesse Worms, foxians, Intellitrons, Wubbaboos, millionaires, Hynes, telepathic spiders, Bavirus, stones, dogs, cats (normal), cats (ragdoll breed)...

t_ch: 箱子和货架，闲人勿近。
t_en: Boxes and shelves. Do not approach.

t_ch: 一排绿色植物，纯粹的摆设。
t_en: A row of greenery purely for decoration.

t_ch: 从湛蓝星直达黑塔的太空电梯。星核猎手入侵事件之后，这座电梯就封闭了。
t_en: The space elevator that goes from The Blue directly to Herta Space Station. It's closed off after the invasion of the Stellaron Hunters.

t_ch: 畸形的黑金色物质在空间站的地面蜿蜒，这是一种被称为「裂界侵蚀」的现象。
t_en: Warped material in black and gold meanders along the floor of the space station, a phenomenon known as Fragmentum corrosion.

t_ch: 星核的爆发会引来「裂界」的侵蚀，它的出现与你体内的星核不无关系。
t_en: Stellaron bursts can lead to Fragmentum corrosion. These are connected to the Stellaron within you.

t_ch: 这点子有意思，不愧是天才俱乐部的人啊。
t_en: Interesting, she's a member of the Genius Society alright.

t_ch: 「星核」就在眼前，抓紧时间。
t_en: The Stellaron is up ahead, let's hurry.

t_ch: 有套独立的防护系统…看来对黑塔而言，「星核」不是寻常的藏品呢。
t_en: It has its own security system... I guess even for Herta, a Stellaron is no ordinary rarity.

t_ch: 取得出来么？
t_en: Can you get it?

t_ch: 当然，在骇客领域，就算天才黑塔也不是我的对手。
t_en: Of course, even the genius Herta can't compete with me when it comes to hacking.

t_ch: 好啊，那载体的准备也拜托你了～
t_en: Good, then I'll also count on you with the preparation of the receptacle.

t_ch: 他还记得多少？
t_en: How much does he remember?

t_ch: 她还记得多少？
t_en: How much does she remember?

t_ch: 至少会记得你。
t_en: ...They'll remember you.

t_ch: 这…是…哪里？
t_en: Where... am I?

t_ch: 这儿？一个空间站，不重要。
t_en: A space station, but that's not important.

t_ch: 卡芙…卡？
t_en: Kafka...?

t_ch: 太好了，你还记得我。
t_en: Great, so you remember me.

t_ch: …你是…谁？
t_en: Who... are you?

t_ch: 不是说会记得我么？
t_en: I thought you'd remember me.

t_ch: 可能你没自己想得那么重要？
t_en: Maybe you're not as important as you think.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>听我说</color>：你的脑袋里现在一片混沌。你不清楚自己是谁，为什么在这儿，接下来要做什么；你觉得我很熟悉，却不清楚该不该信任我——
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Listen</color>: You are in a daze right now. You don't know who you are, why you're here, or what you're going to do next. You think I look familiar, but you're not sure if you should trust me—

t_ch: ——但这都不重要。重要的是我要走了，要把你一个人丢在这个空间站里。所以从现在开始，你不用再思考过去，也不用再怀疑自己。
t_en: ...None of that matters. All you need to know is that I'm leaving, and you will be left all alone on this space station. From now on, you needn't think about your past or doubt yourself.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>听我说</color>：接下来你会遇到很多危险，身处可怕的困境；但你也会遇到许多美妙的事情。你会拥有像家人那样的同伴，开始做梦也想象不到的冒险……
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Listen</color>: In the near future, you will encounter all kinds of perils and hardships, but you will also have many wonderful experiences. You'll meet companions who treat you like family, and embark on surreal adventures with them...

t_ch: 而在旅途的尽头，所有困扰你的谜题都将会解开。
t_en: At the end of your journey, all that perplexes you and troubles you will resolve.

t_ch: 这就是艾利欧所预见的以及你将抵达的未来…喜欢么？
t_en: This is your future that Elio has foreseen. Do you like it?

t_ch: 喜欢…大概。
t_en: I guess...

t_ch: 这就对了。
t_en: Lovely.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>听我说</color>：记住这一刻的感受。只要心中有方向，你就一定能抵达故事的结局。
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Listen</color>: Remember what you're feeling in this moment. As long as you know in your heart where you're heading, you are guaranteed to see the ending of your story.

t_ch: 我就是喜欢这样的你。
t_en: That's the you I like.

t_ch: 我不要…
t_en: No... I don't want it...

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>听我说</color>：艾利欧能窥见未来，却无法干涉你的选择。
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Listen</color>: Elio can foresee the future, but he can't make decisions for you.

t_ch: 以自己的意志抵达那个结局，我就是喜欢这样的你。
t_en: Use that will of yours to reach the end of your story. That's the you I like.

t_ch: 你要去哪…
t_en: Where are you going...

t_ch: 去下一个地方，为准备好的未来铺路。
t_en: The next stop, to pave the way for the future that is written.

t_ch: 这就像织锦一样，你我一次只能编织一条金线，但它最终会变成美丽的图景。
t_en: It's like weaving brocade — you and I can only add one gold thread each time, but eventually, we will make a gorgeous pattern.

t_ch: 还要说多久？按照剧本，{RUBY_B#「开拓」的旅行者}星穹列车{RUBY_E#}的人就快到了，我们不该跟他们照上面。
t_en: How long do you think you need? According to the script, the {RUBY_B#Trailblazers}Astral Express crew{RUBY_E#} is arriving soon. We should avoid being seen by them.

t_ch: 我知道，银狼。再一会儿，就一会儿。
t_en: I know, Silver Wolf. Just give me another minute.

t_ch: 时间快到了，我该走了…<color=#dbc291ff>听我说</color>：很快就会有人找到你，放心跟他们走吧。除我以外，你什么都不记得。
t_en: I must leave now. <color=#dbc291ff>Listen</color>: Don't worry. Someone will come and find you very soon, just go with them. You won't remember a thing except me.

t_ch: 不，不要…
t_en: No...

t_ch: 别了…
t_en: Farewell...

t_ch: 卡芙…卡…
t_en: Kafka...

t_ch: 当你有机会做出选择的时候，不要让自己后悔……
t_en: When you have a chance to make a choice, make one that you know you won't regret...

t_ch: 这也是个很好的答案。
t_en: This is also a lovely answer.

t_ch: 这个人的坐标不是由空间站发出的……
t_en: Weren't their coordinates sent out from the space station...?

t_ch: 都这时候了还计较啥啊。这个大活人就在我们眼前，总不能是假的吧。
t_en: Who cares? They're here and alive. Do they look like a mannequin to you?

t_ch: …心跳和脉搏很微弱，三月，准备做人工呼吸。
t_en: ...Weak heartbeat and pulse... March, you better do CPR.

t_ch: 啊？！我…我没什么经验！丹恒你来吧！
t_en: Huh!? Hehe... I—I've never done it before! Dan Heng, you do it!

t_ch: 停——住口！人醒了！
t_en: Wait — stop it! They're awake!

t_ch: 你没事吧，听得清我说话吗？记不记得自己叫什么名字？
t_en: Are you all right? Can you hear me? Do you remember your name?

t_ch: 我叫{NICKNAME}。
t_en: My name is {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 我…什么都不记得了。
t_en: I... don't remember a thing.

t_ch: …那可麻烦了，能努力回忆一下吗？你的名字是……
t_en: Aw, this isn't good... Can you try harder? I'm sure you can at least remember your name...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}吗？你好，我是丹恒，这是三月七。
t_en: {NICKNAME}? Nice to meet you. My name's Dan Heng, and this is March 7th.

t_ch: 这座空间站遭到了反物质军团的袭击，我们是受艾丝妲站长的委托前来救援的。
t_en: This space station was just attacked by the Antimatter Legion. We came to help with the rescue at the request of Lead Researcher Asta.

t_ch: 反物质军团？
t_en: The Antimatter Legion?

t_ch: 「毁灭」纳努克的打手。你们运气很好，最危险的{RUBY_B#「毁灭」的令使}绝灭大君{RUBY_E#}不在附近，来的只是一群四处作恶的散兵游勇。
t_en: Goons owned by the Destruction. You're lucky that the most dangerous {RUBY_B#Emanator of Destruction}Lord Ravager{RUBY_E#} among them isn't around, just some nasty minions.

t_ch: 我们很快会把入侵者消灭掉的，不用担心。
t_en: We'll take those invaders out soon, don't worry.

t_ch: 艾丝妲站长？
t_en: Lead Researcher Asta?

t_ch: 对呀，粉色头发，个子小小的，很可爱的女孩子。黑塔女士亲自任命的代理站长。
t_en: Uh-huh, a petite cutie with pink hair. Madam Herta appointed her to be the acting lead researcher.

t_ch: …那个孩子也太没威信了，连员工都不知道她名字……
t_en: ...That girl really needs to step up. I can't believe her own employee doesn't even know her name...

t_ch: 我应该去哪…
t_en: Where should I go?

t_ch: 返回主控舱段。艾丝妲站长和及时疏散的研究员都在那里。
t_en: Back to the master control zone. Asta and the other researchers have gathered there.

t_ch: 星穹列车也停在那附近！所以不必担心怪物的袭击，我们会解决这次危机的！
t_en: Plus that's where we parked the Astral Express! Don't you worry, we'll protect you from the monsters and clear up this mess.

t_ch: 你们是谁？
t_en: Who are you guys?

t_ch: 我和丹恒是星穹列车的乘员，请多关照啦！
t_en: Dan Heng and I are members of the Astral Express crew~

t_ch: 列车与黑塔女士有些来往，因此我们时不时会在这里稍作停留。
t_en: Well the Express has some dealings with Madam Herta, so we come to visit the space station from time to time.

t_ch: 结果撞上这么大的事，正义的列车组可不会袖手旁观！
t_en: We just so happened to arrive during this... invasion. Of course, as a — ahem — crew of heroes, we're happy to lend a hand.

t_ch: 星穹列车？
t_en: The Astral Express?

t_ch: 嗯，你没见过吗？列车时不时会来到太空站的。
t_en: Yeah, you've never seen it? The Express comes here every once in a while.

t_ch: 那是一辆神奇的列车，拥有「开拓」星神的力量，能够在星海中穿行。
t_en: It's a unique train that can travel between the stars with the power of the Trailblaze Aeon.

t_ch: 等回到主控舱段，我指给你瞧～
t_en: I'll show you once we're back to the master control zone~

t_ch: 那快走吧。
t_en: Let's go then.

t_ch: 你和三月一起回去吧。
t_en: You and March go back together.

t_ch: 唔，也行，那你自己小心喔。
t_en: Oh, all right. You stay safe.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，要不你把这个拿上吧。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, maybe you should take this.

t_ch: 军团在太空站疯狗似地乱窜，返程的路也不安全，还是带点防身武器为好。
t_en: The Legion are rampaging through the space station like a pack of wolves — this trip won't be a walk in the park. It's better if you have something to protect yourself.

t_ch: 只是个建议啦——没事，你有我呢！
t_en: Just a suggestion though — you're safe as long as you stick with me~!

t_ch: 这里到处都是稀奇的玩意儿，也就是所谓的「奇物」。黑塔收集这些东西又不用，净搁这儿吃灰，也不知道图啥。
t_en: This place is full of fancy little gadgets — Herta's collection. I really don't get why she'd go out of her way to collect them and then leave them here to gather dust...

t_ch: 别看啦，快走快走。
t_en: Enough dilly-dallying, we gotta go.

t_ch: 啊，是反物质军团！这帮疯子流窜过来了，看我上去揍它。
t_en: Ah, it's the Antimatter Legion! So these crazy jerks made it all the way here... Watch this!

t_ch: 人不可貌相啊，你还挺习惯战斗的耶……
t_en: You're stronger than you look... Seem to be quite the fighter.

t_ch: 待会儿我们坐中间那台电梯下行，去主控舱段。那儿的路你熟吗？
t_en: So we'll take that elevator on the central platform to go down to the master control zone. Do you know the way?

t_ch: 呃，说起来你穿的也不是空间站的制服，你真的是这儿的人吗？
t_en: Um, I notice that you're not wearing the space station staff uniform. Do you really work here?

t_ch: 我是。
t_en: Yes.

t_ch: …真的吗？总感觉你在骗我。
t_en: ...Really? Feels like you're tricking me...

t_ch: 我不记得了。
t_en: I don't remember.

t_ch: …失忆了吗？看来还是受了点伤。
t_en: ...Memory loss, huh. You must've been injured.

t_ch: …算了，咱也不问了。
t_en: ...Never mind. I won't ask if you don't wanna talk about it.

t_ch: 走吧，送你回去～
t_en: Let's go, I'll take you to the safe zone~

t_ch: 防卫科的阿兰在这附近失去了联系，我得把他带回去。
t_en: Arlan from the Security Department lost contact in the vicinity. I need to find him first.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}。
t_en: {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 啊，我就知道……
t_en: Ah. I knew it...

t_ch: 成功了？
t_en: It worked?

t_ch: 没，电梯好像损坏了，按键都没反应。
t_en: Nah, I think the elevator's broken. I pressed all the buttons but nothing happened.

t_ch: 弄坏了？
t_en: It's broken?

t_ch: ——不是我干的！一定是反物质军团的错！
t_en: —But it wasn't me! The Antimatter Legion must've broken it!

t_ch: 要是万能的丹恒老师在就好了，他懂一堆莫名其妙的东西，没准连电梯也会修……
t_en: Too bad Dan Heng's not here. He's like a walking encyclopedia... He knows a ton of complicated stuff — maybe even elevator repair...

t_ch: 那个我不会。
t_en: I don't know that one.

t_ch: 哇，你怎么在这儿？！…你怎么比我们还快？
t_en: Whoa! Why are you here!? ...And how did you get here before us?

t_ch: 我走另一条路，从上面一层绕过来的，正好在监控室看见你们。
t_en: I took another route to the upper level, and I saw you guys from up there.

t_ch: 阿兰也在监控室。他受了点伤，但没生命危险。
t_en: Arlan is in the control room. He's been injured, but not fatally.

t_ch: 你找到他了啊…阿兰他知道这电梯怎么回事吗？
t_en: You found him! Will he know what to do about this elevator?

t_ch: 既然是防卫科的负责人，应该知道吧。
t_en: I suppose as the head of the Security Department, he should know.

t_ch: 那我们去和他会合吧！
t_en: Then let's go talk to him!

t_ch: …你们是一起的吗？
t_en: Hey... you're all together?

t_ch: 对呀，我和他都是星穹列车的乘员～
t_en: Yep, we're from the Astral Express~

t_ch: 噢…是黑塔女士请你们来支援这里的么？
t_en: Oh... Did Madam Herta send you to help?

t_ch: 巧合而已。黑塔委托列车寻找的遗器到手了，我们按照协议返回交付…不想正好碰上了遇袭现场。
t_en: It's just a coincidence. We came to deliver the rare relic Herta trusted us to find... We didn't expect to arrive during an invasion.

t_ch: 反物质军团为啥会盯上你们啊？我看它们攻击的目标就是空间站，对星球地表毫无兴趣的样子。
t_en: Why is the Antimatter Legion targeting you guys? It seems they just ignored the surface of the planet and came straight to the space station.

t_ch: 我也…没什么头绪。军团来得十分蹊跷…站内的防卫系统突然失效，没过多久那些家伙就出现了。
t_en: I... have no idea. The Legion came at a very suspicious time — almost right after the security system suddenly failed.

t_ch: 小姐…艾丝妲站长第一时间组织疏散科员，我本该负责掩护所有人撤离，但…没想到最后还是大意了。
t_en: Lady... Lead Researcher Asta immediately began to organize the evacuation. I was supposed to cover everyone as they evacuated, but... I didn't expect to end up failing in this task.

t_ch: 不要自责。你伤了腿和惯用手，藏身在这里避开与军团正面交锋，这个判断十分冷静。
t_en: Don't be too hard on yourself. Your leg and dominant hand were injured. It was a wise decision to hide here and avoid a head-on encounter with the Legion.

t_ch: 对！大部分科员已经去避难了，当务之急是返回主控舱段，组织反击！所以…你知道电梯怎么用吗？它启动不了了……
t_en: Yeah! Most of the staff have been evacuated safely, so right now our highest priority is to return to the master control zone and plan a counterattack! So... do you know how to use the elevator? I couldn't start it...

t_ch: 撤离完成后为了防止军团进攻主控舱段，电梯的权限暂时封锁了。
t_en: After the evacuation was complete, the elevators were all shut down to keep the Legion away from the master control zone.

t_ch: 艾丝妲小姐委托你们来找我，应该有给你们解锁的密钥吧？
t_en: Since Lady Asta sent you to look for me, I assume she must've given you the encryption key for accessing the elevator system?

t_ch: 好像…是给了我一张卡……
t_en: Oh, right, she did give me some sort of card...

t_ch: 三月……
t_en: ...March...

t_ch: 我不记得放哪儿了……
t_en: But where did I put it...

t_ch: …既然密钥找到了就赶快开始吧，电梯的权限用那边的操作台就能解锁。
t_en: ...Now that we found the key, we should get going. We can use that console over there to unlock the elevator.

t_ch: 走吧。
t_en: Let's go.

t_ch: 等一下，我只开启了最上层的电梯口，从那里走。
t_en: Hold on. I only activated the elevator on the highest floor. We'll have to go there to use it.

t_ch: 这么麻烦？
t_en: Why? It's so far away.

t_ch: 绝对不能让军团利用电梯侵入到主控舱段，我必须优先考虑科员们的安全。
t_en: Because I mustn't give the Legion any chance to reach the master control zone by the elevator. I must ensure the safety of the station's staff.

t_ch: 烦请各位多绕一点路…有劳了。
t_en: So I'll have to trouble you to take a bit of a detour... Apologies.

t_ch: 都听你的。
t_en: Okay.

t_ch: 抱歉…空间站是黑塔女士交给小姐的东西，我必须优先考虑主控舱段和科员们的安全。
t_en: Sorry... This space station was entrusted to Lady Asta by Madam Herta. I must do this for the safety of the master control zone and the staff there.

t_ch: 不得不劳烦各位多绕一点路…真的很抱歉。
t_en: So I'll have to trouble you to take a bit of a detour... Sincerest apologies.

t_ch: 什么「各位」啊？你不一起来吗？
t_en: Huh? You're not coming with us?

t_ch: 我行动不便…会拖累你们。我就留在这里，在你们成功抵达后再次封锁电梯权限。
t_en: My leg is injured... I'd only slow you down. I'll stay here and shut down the elevator once you've made it to the master control zone.

t_ch: 你不必担心会拖后腿。封锁电梯权限一事，在主控舱段也可以进行操作吧？
t_en: You won't slow us down. And you should be able to shut down the elevators from the master control zone, right?

t_ch: 就是啊，我和{NICKNAME}两个人不也这么过来了，再加一个丹恒，不至于不至于~
t_en: Agreed. {NICKNAME} and I made it here safely, and now we've also got Dan Heng. We're more than enough to protect you~

t_ch: 反物质军团就交给我们，阿兰你只要跟着我们，顾好自己就行了。
t_en: Let us worry about the Antimatter Legion. You just follow us and keep yourself alive~

t_ch: 相信我们就好。
t_en: Trust us.

t_ch: 不用想太多。
t_en: Don't overthink this.

t_ch: 要不我借你个肩膀？
t_en: You can use me as a crutch.

t_ch: 关心用错地方了吧！
t_en: Aren't you a nice kid!

t_ch: …谢谢。
t_en: ...Thank you.

t_ch: 分拣分析室已经紧急封闭了，那条路走不通。
t_en: The Sorting and Analysis Room has been sealed off. We can't go this way.

t_ch: 你对反物质军团入侵这里的目的有什么头绪么，阿兰？
t_en: Do you know why the Antimatter Legion would invade the station, Arlan?

t_ch: 无非是冲着黑塔女士的藏品来的吧…但都这样了，为什么还……
t_en: They must've come for Madam Herta's collection... But as to why, I still...

t_ch: 珍贵的实验报告被丢得到处都是……
t_en: So many precious lab reports scattered about. What a mess...

t_ch: 斥力桥关闭了？
t_en: The repulsion bridge has been shut down?

t_ch: 嗯，这样多多少少可以拖延敌人一会儿。
t_en: Yes. To stall the enemy, more or less.

t_ch: 这就到电梯口了？一路上也没遇到多少敌人嘛～
t_en: Is that it? We've reached the elevator already? That wasn't too many monsters~

t_ch: 唉，话一出口我就觉得大事不妙……
t_en: Oof, probably shouldn't have said that... Might bring bad luck.

t_ch: 说明你成长了，三月。
t_en: You've grown, March.

t_ch: 这个数量…是不是有点不太妙？
t_en: This number... isn't good, is it?

t_ch: 走！
t_en: Go!

t_ch: 看来还是有好好完成委托呢，欢迎回来。
t_en: It looks like you did well completing that task. Welcome back.

t_ch: 嗯，和我们来时的道路一样。
t_en: Yes, it's the same as when we came.
You...

t_ch: 啊我找到了！
t_en: Ah! Here it is!

t_ch: 我听闻黑塔女士的藏品中有一颗「星核」。
t_en: I heard there's something called a Stellaron in Madam Herta's collection.

t_ch: …那不是我这种身份的人能够接触到的信息。
t_en: ...That's not the kind of information someone in my position would have access to.

t_ch: 喂，这亮晶晶的是…「光锥」吧？这不是星际和平公司内部才有的稀罕货吗？
t_en: Hey, these shiny things... Are these Light Cones? I thought only the Interastral Peace Corporation had rare stuff like this.

t_ch: 不，「光锥」是流光忆庭的技术，由于它能将记忆随身携带，是非常厉害的强化类奇物。据说公司花了大价钱买下了使用权——但黑塔怎么弄到手，就不好说了。
t_en: No, Light Cones are Garden of Recollection technology. They allow you to carry memories around — very potent enhancement items. I heard the corporation paid a lot for the usage rights, but I've no idea how Herta managed to get hold of one.

t_ch: 这几张光锥的来路是正当的，我只能说到这里。你们先带上吧，它对战斗很有帮助。
t_en: They were acquired legitimately, that's all I can say. Take them, they're very useful in combat.

t_ch: 小三月，丹恒，辛苦你们啦。
t_en: March, Dan Heng, you've been through a lot.

t_ch: 姬子～你早点来嘛！最后那票反物质军团和蝗虫似的。咱可是用箭的，打起来多费劲呀。
t_en: Whew, Himeko~ What took you so long! That last wave of Antimatter Legion came at us like a swarm of locusts, have you ever tried shooting locusts with a bow?

t_ch: 来得早也没用啊。我的轨道炮倒是能打掉一片，不过黑塔回来看见空间站这模样，非找我们算账不可。
t_en: I wouldn't have made a difference. My orbital cannon can deal with a whole bunch of enemies at once, but I couldn't just blow up the space station with it — Herta would NOT like that...

t_ch: 你没事吧，阿兰？艾丝妲很担心你。
t_en: Are you alright, Arlan? Asta's been worried about you.

t_ch: 我没什么大碍，伤口包扎下就好。谢谢你们，我先向艾丝妲站长汇报情况去了，再见。
t_en: I'm fine, a quick patching up will do. Thanks for asking. I'll report the situation to Lead Researcher Asta immediately. Bye.

t_ch: 嗨，初次见面，我是姬子，星穹列车的领航员。
t_en: Hey, nice to meet you. I'm Himeko, navigator of the Astral Express.

t_ch: 也就是说，列车怎么行动都听她的。
t_en: In other words, she's in charge of where the Express goes.

t_ch: 这一路过来，小三月没给你添什么麻烦吧？
t_en: March hasn't been any trouble for you along the way, has she?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你想好再回答哦。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, think carefully about how you wanna reply to that.

t_ch: 没见过这么冒失的姑娘。
t_en: I've never met a more reckless girl in my life.

t_ch: ——我就是这个性格嘛！这次又没惹祸…习惯就好了…你看，丹恒他就习惯了。
t_en: ...That's just my charm! And I didn't cause any trouble. You just have to get used to me... Look, Dan Heng agrees, right?

t_ch: 我有权保持沉默。
t_en: I have the right to remain silent.

t_ch: 没见过这么活泼的姑娘。
t_en: I've never met a more lively girl in my life.

t_ch: 嘿，咱是年轻人！就该朝气蓬勃勇往直前嘛！{M#他}{F#她}这个人挺有眼光的，是吧丹恒？
t_en: Well, that's the joy of youth! We should take every step forward full of energy! {M#He's}{F#She's} a discerning one, isn't {M#he}{F#she}, Dan Heng?

t_ch: 我有权保持沉默。
t_en: I have the right to remain silent.

t_ch: 那我还是放弃回答吧。
t_en: In that case, I'd rather not answer.

t_ch: …好像队伍里一下子冒出来两个丹恒。
t_en: ...Oh, looks like we have two Dan Hengs on our team now.

t_ch: 你聊你的，别扯上我。
t_en: Please leave me out of this.

t_ch: 哈哈，年轻人就是容易打成一片，看来你们已经相当熟络了。
t_en: Haha, look at you all, you've already gotten really close.

t_ch: 走吧，艾丝妲可担心你们了。
t_en: Come on, Asta has been worried about all of you.

t_ch: 投射雷达跟踪正常，遥测信号频率偏高！必须维持在平均水平！
t_en: Projectile radar tracking, normal. Telemetry signal frequency unusually high! Maintain at normal levels!

t_ch: 根据预测，军团即将进行十个波次以上的连续袭击，大家扛住！
t_en: Our measurements predict that the Legion is about to unleash over ten waves of continuous attacks. Everyone, brace yourselves!

t_ch: 艾丝妲站长！我们回来啦！
t_en: Asta! We're back!

t_ch: 呼，你们平安回来了就好。阿兰刚刚来过了，收容舱段的事他都告诉我了。还有他的伤…多亏你们的帮助。
t_en: Whew, I'm glad you're all back safe. Arlan just told me about the situation at the storage zone — and about his injury... thank you, for all your help.

t_ch: 灾难当头时，越发体会到科员才是空间站最珍贵的财富…唉，我们对突发事件的准备实在太少了，忽略了安保和战斗人员的建设。
t_en: In times of disaster, I realize more and more that the space station's researchers are its most valuable assets... Alas, we were ill-prepared for such emergencies... we should have built up our security and combat departments.

t_ch: 倒是你们星穹列车的乘员，个个身手不凡……
t_en: On the other hand, the entire crew of the Astral Express seems to be extraordinarily skilled.

t_ch: 空间站当下情况如何？
t_en: What is the current situation on the space station?

t_ch: 目前状况还在控制之中。安保系统受到的破坏很轻微，入侵者只改写了少数核心数据，因此很容易修复。
t_en: The situation is under control for now. The damage to our security system was minor. The intruder only managed to alter a small amount of data, so it was easy to fix.

t_ch: 潜在的风险在于科员们…他们非常信任黑塔女士，根本没想过空间站会被军团攻破。相较于肉体的伤势，精神上的恐慌更加可怕……
t_en: The real problem lies with the researchers... They trust Madam Herta wholeheartedly and never thought that the space station would be breached by the Legion. A broken spirit is far worse than a broken body.

t_ch: 我们去和科员们聊聊吧。这种时候，大家都不希望空间站的内部再出现意外。你试过联系黑塔了吗？
t_en: Let's go speak with the researchers. Right now, the space station can't afford any more unexpected turns, especially from within. Have you tried contacting Herta?

t_ch: 我发了很多封信件，全都石沉大海。姬子小姐了解她的，空间站就是她收容追随者和奇物的仓库，完全没放在心上。
t_en: I sent multiple letters, all met with silence. You know her, Himeko, the space station is but a mere warehouse for followers and rare items. She doesn't really care about it.

t_ch: 我就知道…没关系，我也会向黑塔发信，说她要的奇物我们带来了。至少这个对她还有点吸引力。
t_en: I knew it... No matter. I'll also send a letter to Herta and tell her that we've brought the rare item she seeks. At least that might get her attention.

t_ch: 那就帮大忙了。
t_en: That would be of great help.

t_ch: 科员的安抚就拜托你们了。
t_en: I'll leave the reassurance of the researchers in your hands.

t_ch: 如果有其他疑问，也欢迎来找我和姬子。
t_en: If you have any other questions, you are welcome to come to Himeko and me.

t_ch: 你好。你是…{NICKNAME}，对吗？有什么想知道的？
t_en: Hello, you must be... {NICKNAME}, right? How can I help you?

t_ch: 想了解空间站。
t_en: I'd like to learn about the space station.

t_ch: 这是空间站「黑塔」，其所有权么，当然归属于尊敬的黑塔女士本人。
t_en: This is Herta Space Station. As you might tell from its name, it belongs to the distinguished Madam Herta herself.

t_ch: 「黑塔」原本只是作为奇物和遗器仓库而造的，但黑塔女士允许她的追随者们参与管理和研究，这里就逐渐变成了一座科研基地。
t_en: Herta Space Station was originally built as a warehouse for rare items and relics, but Madam Herta also allowed her followers to oversee tasks and conduct research. It then gradually turned into a scientific research base.

t_ch: 你们经过的收容舱段收藏着诸多「奇物」和「遗器」，是最核心的研究场所。
t_en: The storage zone you passed through contains all our inventory, and is the main research center.

t_ch: 我们现在所在的主控舱段，则是空间站的控制中枢。
t_en: The master control zone where we are now is the control center of the whole space station.

t_ch: 据说空间站里还藏着黑塔女士的私人密室，里面封印着最珍贵，最稀有和最危险的藏品…就她的性格来说，这很有可能……
t_en: It is said that the space station also contains Madam Herta's private chamber where her most precious, rare, and dangerous collections are sealed... And for someone like her, it's not out of the realm of possibility.

t_ch: 很多事她都没告诉我这个「站长」。
t_en: Even from me, the lead researcher, there are many things she keeps secret.

t_ch: 想了解艾丝妲。
t_en: I'd like to learn about you.

t_ch: 我是空间站「黑塔」的站长，但…其实不过是替黑塔女士代为打理的管家罢了。
t_en: I'm lead researcher of Herta Space Station. But honestly... I'm more like Madam Herta's "housekeeper" of this place.

t_ch: 黑塔女士是个天才，不过如何管理各抒己见的科员，如何直接又不失礼貌地代表空间站回复博识学会的种种刁难，这些超出了她的能力范围，但恰好我能处理这一块儿。
t_en: Madam Herta is a genius, but managing disparate staff, tactfully representing the space station before the Intelligentsia Guild... these are all things beyond her abilities. But as it turns out they are my forte.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，学者们可比家里那些蜩螗沸羹的老家伙好对付多啦。
t_en: Hehe, scholars are a lot easier to deal with than those vociferous old geezers back at home.

t_ch: 他们再怎么念叨「天文学只是观测的学问，并不能创造经济价值」，见到黑塔女士还不是大气不敢出？
t_en: No matter how much they drone on about "Astronomy is just about looking at things, it doesn't bring any real value," they all fall silent in front of Madam Herta.

t_ch: 不管什么难题，只要说这是黑塔女士的意思就行。毕竟黑塔本人，就是「智慧」价值的象征。
t_en: No matter the problem, you just needed to say that it was Madam Herta's wishes and all would be fine. After all, Herta herself is a symbol of wisdom.

t_ch: 哎，多亏了她，我才能数着星星睡觉。不然就得回去继承家业了，光想想就觉得可怕极啦。
t_en: Hey, thanks to her, I can count the stars to sleep. Otherwise, I would've had to take over the family fortune. Just thinking about it terrifies me.

t_ch: 想了解阿兰。
t_en: I'd like to learn about Arlan.

t_ch: 阿兰去休息区包扎了。他很有自知之明…要是他还硬撑，我肯定要好好骂他一顿。
t_en: Arlan went to dress his wounds at the rest area. He knows himself well... but if he insists on pushing his limits, I'll give him a good earful.

t_ch: 我真的很生气诶，明明所有科员都完成避难了，他还不服从命令，非要再次搜查收容舱段。
t_en: I'm very upset he didn't obey my orders and insisted on searching the storage zone even though everyone had already evacuated.

t_ch: 我知道他是不想放弃任何一名科员…防卫科对他来说不仅仅是一份工作。
t_en: I know he didn't want to leave any researchers behind... The Security Department is more than just a job to him.

t_ch: 阿兰那家伙根本不在乎受伤，他只在乎自己能不能保护别人，只有这样他才会…感到自己获得了认可。
t_en: But Arlan doesn't care about getting hurt, he only cares about protecting others. It's the only way he feels... fulfilled.

t_ch: 我也不是不理解他的心情，但还是希望他更重视自己啊！
t_en: And it's not that I don't understand where he's coming from. I just want him to look out for himself, too!

t_ch: 想了解黑塔。
t_en: I'd like to learn about Herta.

t_ch: 黑塔女士么…她是「天才俱乐部」的会员，得到「智识」星神青睐的天之骄子。{RUBY_B#「智识」的星神}博识尊{RUBY_E#}诞生以来，这样的人总共也才<unbreak>84</unbreak>个。
t_en: Herta... she's a member of the Genius Society. One of the proud favorites of the Aeon of Erudition. Ever since the birth of {RUBY_B#Aeon of Erudition}Nous{RUBY_E#}, there have only been <unbreak>84</unbreak> people admitted into the Genius Society.

t_ch: 所以，黑塔女士超级任性的…她只做自己感兴趣的事，一旦失去兴趣就立刻走人，这座空间站就是这样的产物……
t_en: That is why Madam Herta is extremely... headstrong... She only pursues what interests her and as soon as that interest is lost, she abandons it and moves on. The space station was one such abandoned interest...

t_ch: 前不久，我借出了一份她的手稿作为资料参考，应该还在主控舱段里，你有兴趣的话也可以看看。
t_en: Anyway, I lent out one of her manuscripts as a resource reference not long ago. It should still be in the master control zone. You can take a look if you wish.

t_ch: 我问完了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 哈哈，和你聊天，我的心情似乎也平静了一些。果然多和人交流，心情就会变好。
t_en: Huh, chatting with you seems to have lightened my mood. It seems like talking can really raise one's spirits.

t_ch: 怎么了？{NICKNAME}，你好像很好奇哦？
t_en: What's the matter, {NICKNAME}? You seem to have something on your mind.

t_ch: 想了解「星穹列车」。
t_en: I'd like to learn about the Astral Express.

t_ch: 是一辆在宇宙中行驶的列车。如果要进一步解释，我会说：「星穹列车」是「开拓」星神阿基维利曾搭乘的列车。
t_en: Basically speaking, it's a train that travels through space. But more specifically, the Astral Express was a creation of the Aeon, Akivili the Trailblazer.

t_ch: 至于开拓、星神、阿基维利分别是什么，等这里的事端平息了再聊——你也不想突然被一大堆名词怼在脸上吧？
t_en: As for what the Trailblaze, the Aeons, and Akivili are all about, let's talk about it when things have calmed down a little. I don't wanna overwhelm you now, do I?

t_ch: 想了解「光锥」。
t_en: I'd like to learn about Light Cones.

t_ch: 「光锥」是流光忆庭的发明，这群「{RUBY_B#浮黎}记忆{RUBY_E#}」星神的追随者通过提炼记忆片段，制成「光锥」，让记忆得以留存。
t_en: Light Cones were created by the Garden of Recollection. These followers of the Aeon of {RUBY_B#Fuli}Remembrance{RUBY_E#} refined memory fragments to create Light Cones, which preserve memories.

t_ch: 你一定想到了吧？「光锥」既然能保存记忆，也就能保存经验和能力。所以「光锥」是非常珍贵的奇物，在{RUBY_B#银河垄断经济体}星际和平公司{RUBY_E#}是二级管制流通商品。
t_en: But you've already realized it, haven't you? Since Light Cones can preserve memories, they can also preserve experiences and abilities. That's why Light Cones are extremely precious rare items under level-2 restriction by the {RUBY_B#Galactic Monopoly}Interastral Peace Corporation{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 据我所知，只有星际和平公司获得了流光忆庭授权的光锥化技术。不知道黑塔和流光忆庭做了什么交易，根据她的性格，多半不是走正规途径弄来的……
t_en: As far as I know, only the IPC possesses Light Cone technology authorized by the Garden of Recollection. I couldn't say what deal Herta made with the Garden, but knowing her, it was likely off the books...

t_ch: 珍惜每一片「光锥」吧。它们对于原主人来说，是非常重要的记忆。
t_en: You should cherish every Light Cone. They are all precious memories of their original owners.

t_ch: 你知道我是谁…吗？
t_en: Do you... know who I am?

t_ch: 唔，我对你的身份一无所知，但我很熟悉你的…气息。
t_en: Who exactly you are escapes me, but you seem familiar to me.

t_ch: 对你嘛，我有些许猜想，但一切都无从佐证，因此先保留这个小小的秘密。
t_en: I have a couple of hunches, but I can't prove any at this point. So, they'll be my little secrets for now.

t_ch: 你很在意自己的过去吗？我倒觉得你是比起过去，更在意未来的人…可能因为我是这样的人吧。
t_en: Are you concerned about your past? You seem like someone who prefers to look forward rather than back... Or maybe I'm just describing myself.

t_ch: 我问完了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 有问题随时来找我哦。
t_en: Come to me anytime if you have more questions.

t_ch: …如果我能早点发现反物质军团……
t_en: ...If only I'd caught the Antimatter Legion sooner...

t_ch: 你还好吗？
t_en: How're you feeling?

t_ch: 我没事，这点疼痛真的不算什么，我早就习惯了。况且为了保护大家而受伤，本来就是一件值得自豪的事。
t_en: I'm fine, this pain is nothing, really. I'm used to it. In fact, I should be proud of it. It's a reminder of being able to protect everyone.

t_ch: 咳，我刚刚说的那些话，你别告诉艾丝妲站长。
t_en: Ahem, but don't tell Lead Researcher Asta what I just said.

t_ch: 空间站情况如何？
t_en: How's the space station?

t_ch: 还是有个别科员遭遇了不幸…
t_en: There were a few researchers who didn't make it...

t_ch: 其他大部分科员只是受了轻伤，但我担心的是他们的精神状态。
t_en: The rest are fine, mostly minor injuries. But I'm worried about their mental states.

t_ch: 大多数人都是仰慕黑塔女士来到空间站的，现在联系不上她，也没有余地让他们联系家人，只希望他们能挺过这次危机。
t_en: Most of them came to the space station because they admired Madam Herta. But now they can't reach her or even their own families. I just hope they can all survive this crisis.

t_ch: 艾丝妲是怎样的人？
t_en: What's Asta like?

t_ch: 我认识艾丝妲站长的时候，她是个大集团的富家小姐。
t_en: My first impression was that she was the daughter of a rich family — owners of a big company.

t_ch: 她对我有恩，所以她去空间站的时候，我也跟来了。
t_en: She was kind to me though. When she went to the space station, I followed her.

t_ch: 我不是很懂科研，但能保护珍视研究的人们顺利完成他们的研究，就足够了。
t_en: I don't know much about science, but I'm glad to be able to protect people in pursuit of their research.

t_ch: 给你拿了些绷带。
t_en: I brought you some bandages.

t_ch: 谢谢，帮大忙了。伤口如果还在渗血，就不能放心抱佩佩了。
t_en: Thank you, I appreciate it. I wouldn't be able to hold Peppy properly if these wounds kept bleeding.

t_ch: 佩佩？
t_en: Peppy?

t_ch: 佩佩是我们的家人，毛茸茸的小狗，就像个白色小雪球。
t_en: Oh, Peppy's a fluffy little puppy, like a white snowball. He's family.

t_ch: 还好灾难发生的时候，它已经躲进自己的小睡眠舱了。
t_en: Luckily, he'd already hidden in his little sleeping pod when the disaster struck.

t_ch: 有空让你见见，你也会喜欢它的。
t_en: Y'know, I'll introduce you when you have the time. You're gonna love him.

t_ch: 约好了。
t_en: I'll hold you to that.

t_ch: 我喜欢狗。
t_en: I love dogs.

t_ch: 我是猫派。
t_en: I'm more of a cat person.

t_ch: 这样啊。
t_en: Huh, I see...

t_ch: 别说话了，好好休息。
t_en: Alright, I'll leave you to get some rest.

t_ch: 希望这场风波尽快过去。
t_en: I hope all this chaos will end soon.

t_ch: 我要走，我要逃，我要离开这里……
t_en: I need to leave, I need to run from this forsaken...

t_ch: 不，我逃不掉的，逃得过昨天，逃得过今天，也逃不过明天……
t_en: No, it's impossible. I can run from yesterday, I can run from today, but tomorrow will always be one step ahead of me...

t_ch: 你看看，障碍判别终端已经记录了<unbreak>142856</unbreak>次攻击，下个数字将是<unbreak>142857</unbreak>！
t_en: Look, the obstacle detection terminal has recorded <unbreak>142,856</unbreak> attacks... the next count will be <unbreak>142,857</unbreak>!

t_ch: 我坚定地相信着，等我看到了这个绝望的美丽的走马灯数，我的人生也走到了走马灯的最后……
t_en: I know in my heart that when I see that hopelessly beautiful cyclic number... it will mean the poetic end of my life's cycle.

t_ch: 会好起来的。
t_en: Things will get better.

t_ch: 每个人都这么说，「会好起来的」，「不会总这么倒霉」……
t_en: Everyone keeps saying that. "Things will get better, things won't always be this bad."

t_ch: 否定…肯定…否定的否定即是肯定。
t_en: Negative... positive... A negative of a negative is a positive.

t_ch: 做个善良的人吧，年轻人，说实话吧！
t_en: Why not do the right thing and let me hear the truth!?

t_ch: 人总是要死的，但不是现在。
t_en: Life is but a path to death, but not today.

t_ch: 「人总是要死的，但不是现在」…多么有哲理的话语，没错，当你愈接近「死」时，愈会意识到当下才是「生」的时刻！
t_en: Life is but a path to death, but not today... How insightful. Yes, of course! The closer you approach death, the more you appreciate life.

t_ch: 我要打起精神来，谢谢你，好心人。
t_en: I will pull myself together. Thank you, that was kind of you.

t_ch: 人总是要死的，但不是现在。
t_en: Life is but a path to death, but not today.

t_ch: 多么有哲理的话语，没错，当你愈接近「死」时，愈会意识到当下才是「生」的时刻！
t_en: How insightful, yes of course. The closer you approach death, the more you appreciate life.

t_ch: 我要打起精神来，谢谢你，好心人。
t_en: I will pull myself together. Thank you, that was kind of you.

t_ch: 「人总是要死的，但不是现在。」谢谢，素未相识的好心人。
t_en: Life is but a path to death, but not today... Thank you, kind stranger.

t_ch: 推下摇杆，阿尔弗瑞德…抱歉抱歉，我认错人了。
t_en: Push the stick down, Alfred... Sorry, sorry, I got the wrong person.

t_ch: 我来帮你。
t_en: Let me help.

t_ch: 麻烦你了，年轻人，我们在进行卫星遥感的预调试。我按住监测信号的按钮，你按我说的步骤来推摇杆。
t_en: Thanks, kid. We're doing some pre-checks for the satellite remote sensing. I'll hold down the button to monitor the signal, and you push the control stick according to my instructions.

t_ch: 先说摇杆的方向，「上」是频谱仪，「下」是滤波器，「左」是参考电平，「右」是衰耗器。
t_en: But first let's talk about the controls. "Up" is the spectrometer, "Down" is the filter, "Left" is the reference level, and "Right" is the attenuator.

t_ch: 我们的预调试有三个步骤，打开频谱仪，再是衰耗器，最后把信号输出到滤波器上。
t_en: Our pre-checks have three steps. Turning on the spectrometer, then the attenuator, and finally outputting the signal to the filter.

t_ch: 内容有点多…都记住了么？
t_en: It's quite a bit to remember... You got everything?

t_ch: 记住了。
t_en: Got it.

t_ch: 没记住。
t_en: Could you repeat that again?

t_ch: 那我再说一遍。
t_en: Don't worry, I'll go over it again.

t_ch: 摇杆的方向，「上」是频谱仪，「下」是滤波器，「左」是参考电平，「右」是衰耗器。
t_en: The controls are "Up" for the spectrometer, "Down" for the filter, "Left" for the reference level, and "Right" for the attenuator.

t_ch: 预调试有三个步骤，打开频谱仪，再是衰耗器，最后把信号输出到滤波器上。
t_en: The pre-checks have three steps. Turning on the spectrometer, then the attenuator, and finally outputting the signal to the filter.

t_ch: 准备好了么？三，二，一……
t_en: Are you ready? Three, two, one...

t_ch: 上，下，左
t_en: Up, down, left

t_ch: 顺序不对，年轻人。我给你演示一下。
t_en: Wrong order, kid. Let me demonstrate.

t_ch: 摇杆向上打开频谱仪，再向右控制衰耗器，最后是向下输出到滤波器。
t_en: Push the control stick up to activate the spectrometer, then right for the attenuator, and finally down for the filter.

t_ch: 下，上，右
t_en: Down, up, right

t_ch: 顺序不对，年轻人。我给你演示一下。
t_en: Wrong order, kid. Let me demonstrate.

t_ch: 摇杆向上打开频谱仪，再向右控制衰耗器，最后是向下输出到滤波器。
t_en: Push the control stick up to activate the spectrometer, then right for the attenuator, and finally down for the filter.

t_ch: 上，右，下
t_en: Up, right, down

t_ch: 完美，谢谢。
t_en: Wonderful, thank you.

t_ch: 帮大忙了，年轻人。原本负责这块的科员阿尔弗瑞德，他刚刚在收容舱段…没有回来。
t_en: That was very helpful of you, kid. Alfred was the researcher in charge of this — he was in the storage zone earlier... He didn't make it back.

t_ch: 亚伯拉罕跟他关系不错，我还不知道怎么跟他说这件事…哎。
t_en: Abraham was close to him. I don't know how to tell him about it...

t_ch: 当务之急是保住空间站，先专注于眼前的工作吧。
t_en: The top priority is to preserve the space station and concentrate on the work at hand.

t_ch: 好像没有需要帮助的科员了，回艾丝妲那边吧。
t_en: Looks like that's all the researchers who need help. Let's go check in with Asta.

t_ch: 请受伤的科员切勿随意移动，医护人员马上就到…插入一则紧急通知。
t_en: All injured researchers, please stay where you are. Medical staff will be arriving immediately... Here's an emergency notice.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}{M#先生}{F#小姐}，三月七小姐，丹恒先生，听到广播后请速至主控制台，艾丝妲站长正在找你们。
t_en: Would {M#Mr.}{F#Ms.} {NICKNAME}, Ms. March 7th, and Mr. Dan Heng please proceed immediately to the master control station. Lead Researcher Asta is waiting for you.

t_ch: 警告，防护罩受损，<unbreak>55%</unbreak>，<unbreak>73%</unbreak>…主控舱段通道即将封闭。
t_en: Warning: Defense shield damage level at <unbreak>55%</unbreak>... <unbreak>73%</unbreak>... Master control zone passageways will soon be closed.

t_ch: 艾丝妲！
t_en: Asta!

t_ch: 别管我们了，从那边走！
t_en: Don't worry about us. Leave from there!

t_ch: 快！
t_en: Quick!

t_ch: 为什么偏偏在我负责期间，电离网被怪物突破了……
t_en: Why did those monsters have to breach the ionizing grid on my watch...

t_ch: 不是我的错，我不可能犯这种错误…我再去外围确认一遍数据！
t_en: It can't be my fault, I'd never make a mistake like this... I'm going out there to check the data again!

t_ch: 你受伤了。
t_en: You're injured.

t_ch: 受伤？我都不在乎，也没有人在乎。你是谁，别挡我的路。
t_en: Injured? I don't care, see anyone else that cares? Who are you anyway, get out of my way.

t_ch: 外面很危险。
t_en: It's dangerous outside.

t_ch: 危险？当然危险，我刚从最前线回来，能不清楚吗。
t_en: Dangerous? Of course it's dangerous, I just came back from the front line, I know that better than you.

t_ch: 别逞英雄。
t_en: Don't be a hero.

t_ch: 英雄？你想多了，我不为了别人，我是为了自己。
t_en: Hero? Don't overthink it. I'm not doing this for others, I'm doing it for myself.

t_ch: 空间站防护罩的第一道系统名为反重力电离网。我就是负责维护的科员，出了这种事，你指望我能心安理得呆在这里？
t_en: The space station shield's first line of defense is the anti-gravity ionizing grid. I'm the researcher in charge of it. Do you expect me to just sit here after something like this?

t_ch: 这不是你的错。
t_en: It's not your fault.

t_ch: 我也这么认为，但事后追究起来，全责一定在我啊。
t_en: I agree. But when the investigation's over, the blame will fall entirely on my shoulders.

t_ch: 现在已经晚了。
t_en: It's over now.

t_ch: 也许吧，但总比躲在这里什么也不干要好。
t_en: That may be the case, but it's better than just hiding here doing nothing.

t_ch: 乖乖呆在这里。
t_en: Please, just stay here.

t_ch: 哼，这事是没发生在你身上，我可呆不住！
t_en: Hmph... it's easy for you to say! This didn't happen on your watch, you wouldn't understand!

t_ch: 而且，我照看的那帮捣蛋鬼还留在外面没人管…我知道你拦我是好意，但我已经决定了。
t_en: Besides, that bunch of troublemakers is still out there... I know you mean well, but I've made up my mind.

t_ch: 现在，我们所有科员都聚集到这里避难。但说真的，有没有人考虑过，空间站那些非人生物怎么办？
t_en: We researchers have all taken refuge here, but has anyone given any thought to the non-human life on this space station?

t_ch: 它们是奇物藏品没错，但它们就不是生命吗？它们的权益就活该得不到保障吗？！
t_en: They may be part of the rare item collection, but are they not also living things? Don't their rights deserve to be protected!?

t_ch: 非人生物？
t_en: Non-human life?

t_ch: 对，非、人、生、物。就是与人类种族不同的生命！
t_en: Yes. Non. Human. Life. Living creatures that aren't human...

t_ch: 其中有一些作为奇物被收容在空间站内，但几乎所有科员都会漠视它们的基本权益。
t_en: ...Some of which have been collected as rare items and housed in the space station. Most of the researchers disregard their basic rights.

t_ch: 如此生死存亡之秋，他们的目光还是那么狭隘。
t_en: Even now, with death staring them in the face, they still refuse to open their minds.

t_ch: 而我，「黑塔•银河法政科」的优秀科员卢卡奇，有责任叫醒那些装睡的人！
t_en: And I, Lukacs, outstanding researcher of the Herta Department of Galactic Geopolitics, must awaken those pretending to sleep!

t_ch: 奇物藏品？
t_en: Rare item collection?

t_ch: 我们空间站，历来收藏着银河数不胜数的奇物宝贝。但不是所有人都知道，在这藏品之中，也包含着一些活生生的生命！
t_en: At the space station, we've always collected rare items — countless articles from across the galaxy. But not everyone knows that among these articles are also living things.

t_ch: 而我，「黑塔•银河法政科」的优秀科员卢卡奇，有必要向所有人强调，在这生死存亡的危机时刻，它们的生命权益也应该得到尊重！
t_en: And I, Lukacs, outstanding researcher of the Herta Department of Galactic Geopolitics, must emphasize in this time of crisis that their lives and rights should also be respected!

t_ch: 权益保障？
t_en: Protecting their rights?

t_ch: 没错！在下是「黑塔•银河法政科」的优秀科员卢卡奇，在这生死存亡的危机时刻，仍旧为了所有非人生物的生命权益而奔走！疾呼！
t_en: Exactly! And I, Lukacs, outstanding researcher of the Herta Department of Galactic Geopolitics, shall continue fighting and speaking up for the rights of all non-human life in these terrible times!

t_ch: 如果你也有志加入我的事业，就请站在我的身边，与我一同摇旗呐喊罢！
t_en: If you are interested in joining the cause, please stand by my side and repeat after me!

t_ch: 保护奇物权益！拯救非人生物！
t_en: Protect rare item rights! Save non-human life!

t_ch: 一起来！保护奇物权益——
t_en: All together! Protect rare item rights...!

t_ch: 保护奇物权益！
t_en: Protect rare item rights!

t_ch: 做得很好，同行者！跟着我的节奏，声音再大一点，给气势加把柴。
t_en: You did well, friend! Follow my rhythm, and say it loud, with conviction!

t_ch: 保护奇物权益！拯救非人生物！
t_en: Protect rare item rights! Save non-human life!

t_ch: 拯救非人生物！
t_en: Save non-human life!

t_ch: 太完美了，同行者。让我们携手共进，邀请更多的人来加入我们吧！
t_en: Perfect, my friend. Now let us work together and invite more supporters to join us!

t_ch: 保护奇物权益！拯救非人生物！
t_en: Protect rare item rights! Save non-human life!

t_ch: 那我就先走了。
t_en: I think I should leave.

t_ch: 啊！又是一个精致的利己主义者！狭隘的人类中心主义者！
t_en: Hmph! Just another egocentric anthropocentrist!

t_ch: 睁开你浑浊的眼睛吧，看看我们身边的小生命吧——
t_en: Open your benighted eyes and look at the delicate life around you!

t_ch: 保护奇物权益！拯救非人生物！
t_en: Protect rare item rights! Save non-human life!

t_ch: 没错…没错，「怨灵的痴缠化作有形之手，旧日的灾影凝为掩映诸天之尾」！
t_en: That's right... that's right. "The resentful spirit's senseless obsession turns into a tangible hand, and the shadow of the old disaster will conceal the heavens!"

t_ch: 与星神密卷上所书别无二致！那就是「毁灭」纳努克的追随者，以反物质镕铸而成的银河战争兵器。
t_en: That's exactly what was written on the Mystic Scroll of Aeons. It refers to the followers of Nanook the Destruction using antimatter to create a galactic weapon of war.

t_ch: 没想到我有幸亲眼见证，嘿，嘿嘿……
t_en: I didn't think I'd be fortunate enough to witness... hehehehe...

t_ch: 嘘，你也来瞻仰星神的伟力与神迹吗？那你过去点，去，那边还有个好位置。
t_en: Oh, have you also come to witness the power and miracle of the Aeons? Then go over there, there's a good spot right there.

t_ch: 要谨防肉身观测时的交叉干扰，以免错过对细节的捕捉。星神示现，就算是蛛丝马迹，也万万不可放过。嘿，嘿嘿……
t_en: Beware of any interferences from your mortal body when observing... you must seize every detail. When an Aeon appears, even the tiniest trace must not be missed. Hehehe...

t_ch: 希拉，你看到了吗？历史总是在重演。
t_en: You see that, Sheila? History always repeats itself.

t_ch: 历史？古恩，空间站面对如此大的动荡，我还是第一次看见。
t_en: History? Gunn, this is the first time I have witnessed the space station in such disarray.

t_ch: 希拉，你会回来救我们吗？在我们也遇到了同等危难的时候，如果当年你成功挽救了母星……
t_en: Oh Sheila, when we face another dire crisis like this, if you're able to rescue your home planet... Will you come back and save us?

t_ch: 母星？我的母星？…我不明白您所说的意思。
t_en: Home planet? My home planet? ...I do not understand what you are saying.

t_ch: 没有关系。希拉如果能听到，她会明白的。
t_en: Never mind. If Sheila could hear this, she'd understand.

t_ch: 埃美丽，冷静下来，想想<color=#dbc291ff>她</color>遇到这种灾难会怎么做，十杯咖啡？一粒药片？还是躲到堆满星神密卷的房间里？
t_en: Emily, calm down and think about what <color=#dbc291ff>she</color> would do in such a disaster. Ten cups of coffee? A pill? Or maybe hide in a room full of Mystic Scrolls of Aeons?

t_ch: …这咖啡好苦，苦死了。
t_en: ...That's bitter coffee... *coughing* ...so bitter.

t_ch: 苦死了！
t_en: Too dang bitter!!!

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>姐姐</color>，你在哪儿，我真的好害怕……
t_en: Oh <color=#dbc291ff>sis</color>, where are you? I'm so scared...

t_ch: 莱斯莉！师傅，我得出去，我得去给莱斯莉发消息！
t_en: Lesley! Sir, I need to go. I need to go find Lesley.

t_ch: 臭小子，头脑不清醒是吧？你要想出去，先从我这把老骨头上跨过去！
t_en: Stupid boy, is your head not screwed on right? You're leaving over my dead body.

t_ch: 看我不好好揍你几下，别逃，你……
t_en: I'll knock some sense into you yet... Hey, where do you think you're go— Argh...

t_ch: 呜呜，该怎么办呀，刚刚他们说，收容舱段也有怪物，我给<color=#dbc291ff>波少</color>的礼物还留在应物科的分析室里……
t_en: *crying* What should we do!? They said there are monsters in the storage zone. And the gift I prepared for <color=#dbc291ff>Master Capote</color> is still in the analysis room of the Department of Implement Arts...

t_ch: 我跟你说，这次袭击空间站的怪物多达数十种，还有一些是站内谱系都没收录的新型造物——
t_en: You know, there's loads of monsters attacking the space station this time, and some of them aren't even on the station's records—

t_ch: 呜呜呜，你烦死了，我不想听那些，呜哇哇！
t_en: *crying* You're so annoying, stop saying that! *crying*

t_ch: 哎呀，你都多少岁的人了，还像个小孩子！
t_en: Hey, how old are you? Don't be a baby!

t_ch: 能不能别哭了？我给你扮怪物好不好？
t_en: Please stop crying. *gasp* How about I pretend to be a monster for you, huh?

t_ch: 呜啊——
t_en: Raaaawr!
Waaaah!

t_ch: 「奇物编号<unbreak>752</unbreak>：定分枪。该枪会给视线范围内的生物评分，打分逻辑不明。」
t_en: "Item number <unbreak>752</unbreak>: Rating Pistol. This weapon rates creatures on sight, using unknown scoring logic."

t_ch: 这把奇怪的枪闭着的眼陡然睁开，滴溜溜乱转了片刻，不怀好意地盯着你。
t_en: The closed eye of this weird little gun suddenly snaps open as it swirls around for a moment, staring at you with malice.

t_ch: 「4分！」它宣布。眼睛猛然一睁，倍感恐惧，瑟缩不安：「<unbreak>100</unbreak>分！<unbreak>1000</unbreak>分…？<unbreak>10000</unbreak>分……？」
t_en: "4 points!" It announces. As its eyes suddenly snap open, you see a shiver of fear and uncertainty run through it: "<unbreak>100</unbreak> points! <unbreak>1,000</unbreak> points...? <unbreak>10,000</unbreak> points...?"

t_ch: 眼睛疲惫不堪，假装活力满满：「分数无边，无法测定！」终于，它获得了合上眼皮的机会。
t_en: Though the eye is burning with exhaustion, it pretends to be brimming with energy: "The score is limitless. Rating invalid!" Finally, it gets an opportunity to close its eye.

t_en: The Rating Pistol gazes at Aglaea: "<unbreak>99.11</unbreak> points!" it announces.

t_ch: 眼睛的视线飘忽不定，认真而尖锐，像是在探索一片未知的测算领域。
t_en: Its gaze darts around without settling for a moment. The look in its eye is serious and alert as if it's examining a completely unknown realm.

t_en: The Rating Pistol gazes at Tribbie: "<unbreak>90.91</unbreak> points!" it announces.

t_en: The gun's gaze sweeps back and forth with measuring intent. It seems to have gleaned answers hidden somewhere deep. "<unbreak>0.011</unbreak> points!" It declares.

t_ch: 定分枪的眼睛看着万敌：「<unbreak>0.59</unbreak>？」眼睛流露出七分疑惑，三分怀疑。
t_en: The Rating Pistol gazes at Mydei: "<unbreak>0.59</unbreak> points?" the gaze reveals a look of confusion and semi-suspicion.

t_ch: 半晌，眼睛恍然瞪大不再迷茫，坚定而有力。「<unbreak>0.59</unbreak>！」它宣布。
t_en: After a while, the gun's gaze suddenly widens and its confusion is dispelled. "<unbreak>0.59</unbreak>!" it declares, resolutely.

t_en: The Rating Pistol gazes at Anaxa: "<unbreak>0.616</unbreak> points!" it announces.

t_ch: 眼睛无序地转动，最终眯成了弯月形状。它是如此满意这个结果。
t_en: The eye spins without order, before eventually squinting into the shape of a crescent moon. The gun shows great satisfaction at this result.

t_ch: 定分枪的眼睛看着遐蝶：「<unbreak>0.1</unbreak>分！」它宣布。
t_en: The Rating Pistol gazes at Castorice: "<unbreak>0.1</unbreak> point!" it announces.

t_ch: 忽然，眼睛一动不动，「<unbreak>0.09</unbreak>分…<unbreak>0.4012</unbreak>分…<unbreak>0.7007</unbreak>分…」。它沉浸在数据的海洋，不断推翻、重置，仿佛时间已然停滞成了永恒。
t_en: Suddenly, the eye stops moving. "<unbreak>0.09</unbreak> points... <unbreak>0.4012</unbreak> points... <unbreak>0.7007</unbreak> points..." It drowns in a sea of data, constantly updating and reset, as if time was frozen, locked in perpetuity.

t_ch: 定分枪的眼睛看着风堇：「<unbreak>0.35</unbreak>分！」它宣布。
t_en: The Rating Pistol gazes at Hyacine: "<unbreak>0.35</unbreak> points!" it announces.

t_ch: 眼睛流露出明媚地笑意，仿佛连周遭无趣的空气都变得精彩起来。
t_en: The eye sparkles with radiant joy, as if even the dull air around has turned vibrant.

t_ch: 定分枪的眼睛看着赛飞儿：「<unbreak>0.894</unbreak>分！」它宣布。
t_en: The Rating Pistol gazes at Cipher: "<unbreak>0.894</unbreak> points!" it announces.

t_ch: 眼睛上看下看左看右看，敏锐捕捉着每一丝信息，生怕略过了什么。
t_en: The eye scans up, down, left, and right, keenly catching every detail, afraid to miss a thing.

t_ch: 定分枪的眼睛看着白厄：「……？！」
t_en: The Rating Pistol gazes at Phainon: "...!?"

t_ch: 眼睛瞪大，它颤动着感知、测算，拼尽全力。「无法测定！」眼睛似乎受到了强烈的刺激，它陡然紧闭，不再执着于无解的答案。
t_en: Its eyes fly wide open, trembling as it strains to sense and calculate. "Rating invalid!" Overwhelmed, the eyes snap shut, giving up on the impossible answer.

t_ch: 「奇物编号<unbreak>148</unbreak>：螺中笑声。」
t_en: "Item number <unbreak>148</unbreak>: Crackup Conch."

t_ch: 海螺发出奇异的声响，不像笑声，倒是挺好笑的。
t_en: This conch makes a strange sound. It is unlike laughter, but is nevertheless quite humorous.

t_ch: 黑色的羽翼在气流中轻旋，保持着飞翔的样子，长度与一人同高。你想象一位冒险者的身影，挥舞双翼，乘风而起。
t_en: A pair of black feathered wings hovers in the air at about the height of a grown human, maintaining the appearance of flight. You imagine the figure of an adventurer flapping those wings and rising with the wind.

t_ch: 你感受到了风，和煦的风迎面而来，吹动发梢，盈满全身。风送着你飞越高山，远渡诸海，身后是风车转动，牧歌隽永，远方是流星划落，白雪皑皑……
t_en: You feel the wind — A warm and welcoming wind blows into your face, plays with the ends of your hair, and fills your body. The wind sends you to fly beyond mountain ranges and vast oceans. Behind you, windmills turn and pastoral hymns chime. Before you, shooting stars stream down and snow glitters...

t_ch: <unbreak>40</unbreak>张纸牌以精密的布置叠成尖塔的形状。轻轻抽走任何一张，这座纸牌塔便会轰然倒塌。
t_en: <unbreak>40</unbreak> cards are stacked in a precise arrangement to form a spire. Pull away even a single card, and it will collapse into a ruinous heap.

t_ch: 或许世间因果也是如此，选择众多，结局却只有一种。但你相信，偶然寓于必然之中，终有一日，你会找到命定之外的解答。
t_en: Perhaps this is how causality works. We have thousands of choices, but there is only one outcome. And yet, you believe that happenstances are etched in inevitability. One day, you'll find the answer beyond your guaranteed destiny.

t_ch: 几块深红的碎片悬浮在空中，被一股看不见的力量牵引着，勾勒出断刃的轮廓。斑驳的表面像是烈火燃尽的荒原，诉说着它的曾经。
t_en: A few crimson fragments float in the air. Drawn by an invisible force, they trace the shape of a broken blade. The rusted surface looks like a barren field scorched by flames as it narrates its past.

t_ch: 你仔细端详这些碎片，空气中忽然传来一阵微热，拂过脸颊，在你的耳畔驻足停步。
t_en: You closely examine these fragments. A gust of heat suddenly arrives through the air. It touches your cheeks and pauses next to your ear.

t_ch: 你听见有谁在啜泣，有谁在喧嚣，有谁留下无言的哀悼。最后，你听见一声低语——
t_en: You hear someone sobbing, someone yelling, and someone mourning wordlessly. Finally, someone whispers...

t_ch: 「奇物编号<unbreak>2691</unbreak>：热量黑洞。能吸收热量，但不包括食物。」
t_en: "Curio No. <unbreak>2691</unbreak>: Energy Black Hole. It can absorb energy, but not food."

t_ch: 金属外壳上有可疑的油光，看来奇物简介里的后半句是最近才添加上去的。
t_en: There is a suspiciously oily sheen over the metal casing. It seems like the part about food in the Curio introduction was added recently.

t_ch: 「奇物编号<unbreak>021</unbreak>：勘星钎。这款是原型，再强调一遍，原型。」
t_en: "Curio No. <unbreak>021</unbreak>: Astro Rod. This is the PROTOTYPE. I repeat, PROTOTYPE."

t_ch: 量产后变得平平无奇的勘星钎。
t_en: Astro Rods have become so common after being mass-produced.

t_ch: 「奇物编号<unbreak>458</unbreak>：不可承受之重。约特伍德体皇族的桂冠，用来传承知识。」
t_en: "Curio No. <unbreak>458</unbreak>: Unbearable Weight. It is the crown of the Tottonid royal family, used to pass on knowledge."

t_ch: 你试着佩戴这顶王冠，戴上后只感觉头大——因为它实在太重了，看来知识的分量可不轻。
t_en: You try on the crown, and you feel as though your head has grown several times bigger. The crown is far too heavy. Looks like knowledge is pretty dense.

t_ch: 「奇物编号<unbreak>269</unbreak>：分装快乐。立方体内的便携装置，可以用来识别并收集『快乐』类情绪。」
t_en: "Curio No. <unbreak>269</unbreak>: Joy Filtration Set. The portable device inside the cube can be used to identify and collect joyous emotions."
